content_en:
Abu al-Fath Muhammad ibn Abd al-Karim ash-Shahrastani was born around 479 AH (1086 CE) in Shahrastan, a town in the Khorasan region of present-day Turkmenistan. He received his early education in his hometown before traveling to Nishapur, which was then one of the premier centers of Islamic scholarship in the eastern Muslim world. There he studied under some of the most distinguished scholars of his era, including Imam al-Haramayn al-Juwayni's students and the followers of the Ash'ari theological tradition.

Shahrastani lived during a period of remarkable intellectual ferment. The Seljuk Empire provided relative political stability to much of the eastern Muslim world, and the great madrasa system established by the vizier Nizam al-Mulk was producing scholars of extraordinary breadth. Theological and philosophical debates between Mu'tazilites, Ash'aris, Maturidis, and various Shi'a factions were vigorous and sometimes bitter. Meanwhile, the translation movement had made Greek philosophical texts widely available, and scholars like al-Farabi and Ibn Sina had constructed elaborate systems that blended Aristotelian philosophy with Islamic theology.

Shahrastani himself was a nuanced thinker who combined Ash'ari theological commitments with genuine curiosity about the full range of human religious and philosophical opinion. He studied in Baghdad and reportedly served as a preacher there for three years before returning to Khorasan. Some medieval biographers noted that he showed sympathy for Ismaili thought in his later years, a claim that remains debated among modern scholars. He died around 548 AH (1153 CE).

The composition of Al-Milal wan-Nihal came at a time when Muslim scholars were increasingly encountering diverse religious communities — Zoroastrians, Manichaeans, Jews, Christians of various denominations, and the multiplying sects within Islam itself. The Crusades had brought renewed contact with Latin Christianity, while the Fatimid Ismaili caliphate in Cairo represented a significant Shi'a political and intellectual presence. Understanding these groups was not merely an academic exercise but a practical necessity for rulers, judges, and theologians navigating a religiously plural world.

Shahrastani's position in Seljuk intellectual life gave him access to diverse scholarly traditions. He was familiar with the major works of kalam (scholastic theology), philosophy, and earlier heresiographical literature. His teacher Sharaf al-Islam al-Isfaraini had worked in the tradition of earlier heresiographers like al-Baghdadi, but Shahrastani aimed to go further — not merely cataloguing and refuting deviant groups but achieving a comprehensive, relatively objective survey of all known religious and philosophical positions worldwide.
content_id:
Abu al-Fath Muhammad ibn Abd al-Karim ash-Shahrastani lahir sekitar tahun 479 H (1086 M) di Shahrastan, sebuah kota di wilayah Khorasan yang kini menjadi bagian Turkmenistan. Ia menerima pendidikan awal di kampung halamannya sebelum merantau ke Nishapur, yang pada masa itu merupakan salah satu pusat keilmuan Islam terkemuka di dunia Muslim bagian timur. Di sana ia belajar kepada beberapa ulama paling terkemuka di zamannya, termasuk murid-murid Imam al-Haramayn al-Juwayni dan para pengikut mazhab aqidah Asy'ariyah.

Shahrastani hidup pada masa kegairahan intelektual yang luar biasa. Kekaisaran Seljuk memberikan stabilitas politik yang relatif bagi sebagian besar dunia Muslim bagian timur, dan sistem madrasah besar yang dibangun oleh wazir Nizam al-Mulk tengah melahirkan para ulama dengan wawasan yang sangat luas. Perdebatan aqidah dan filsafat antara kaum Mu'tazilah, Asy'ariyah, Maturidiyah, dan berbagai kelompok Syi'ah berlangsung sengit dan terkadang penuh perselisihan. Sementara itu, gerakan penerjemahan telah membuat teks-teks filsafat Yunani tersebar luas, dan para pemikir seperti al-Farabi serta Ibn Sina telah membangun sistem-sistem pemikiran yang memadukan filsafat Aristoteles dengan ilmu kalam Islam.

Shahrastani sendiri adalah seorang pemikir yang sangat teliti, memadukan komitmen aqidah Asy'ariyah dengan rasa ingin tahu yang tulus terhadap seluruh ragam pemikiran keagamaan dan filsafat manusia. Ia belajar di Baghdad dan dikabarkan pernah berkhutbah di sana selama tiga tahun sebelum kembali ke Khorasan. Beberapa biografi abad pertengahan mencatat bahwa ia menunjukkan simpati terhadap pemikiran Isma'iliyah pada masa tuanya, suatu klaim yang hingga kini masih diperdebatkan di kalangan para sarjana modern. Ia wafat sekitar tahun 548 H (1153 M).

Penulisan Al-Milal wan-Nihal berlangsung di saat para ulama Muslim semakin banyak bersinggungan dengan komunitas-komunitas beragama yang beragam: kaum Zoroastrian, Mani, Yahudi, Nasrani dari berbagai denominasi, dan semakin banyaknya sekte-sekte di dalam Islam sendiri. Perang Salib membawa kontak baru yang lebih intens dengan Kekristenan Latin, sementara kekhalifahan Isma'ili Fatimiyah di Kairo merupakan kehadiran politik dan intelektual Syi'ah yang signifikan. Memahami kelompok-kelompok ini bukan sekadar latihan akademis, melainkan suatu kebutuhan praktis bagi para penguasa, hakim, dan teolog yang harus mengarungi dunia yang beragam secara agama.

Kedudukan Shahrastani dalam kehidupan intelektual Seljuk memberinya akses ke berbagai tradisi keilmuan. Ia memiliki pengetahuan mendalam tentang karya-karya utama ilmu kalam, filsafat, dan literatur heresografi sebelumnya. Gurunya, Sharaf al-Islam al-Isfaraini, telah bekerja dalam tradisi para heresografer terdahulu seperti al-Baghdadi, namun Shahrastani bertekad melangkah lebih jauh: bukan sekadar mendaftar dan membantah kelompok-kelompok yang menyimpang, melainkan menghasilkan survei yang komprehensif dan relatif objektif atas semua posisi keagamaan dan filosofis yang dikenal di seluruh dunia.
